title: BL-series Battle Legionnaire

{{Quote|To our BLs, that they may leave enough enemies for the rest of us! ''Kote!|A [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protector's]] [[Toast (ritual)/Legends|toast]]|<ref name="NEGTD" />}}
The '''BL-series Battle Legionnaires''' were a series of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] produced by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] to be used by the [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Though they were manufactured in a limited, one thousand-unit run only, Battle Legionnaire [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were powerful and lethal in combat. Visually similar in design to [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica's]] [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series]] and [[TC-series protocol droid/Legends|TC-series]], their deceptively strong metallic bodies were armed with [[AAP-II blaster box]]es and decorated in the same [[Color/Legends|green]] livery that was commonly seen on the [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|armor]] of the Mandalorian Protectors.

Designed in part by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Fenn Shysa]], the Battle Legionnaire droids were bequeathed to the Mandalorian Protectors by the Confederacy following it joining Separatist [[Separatist Senate/Legends|government]]. The war droids bolstered the Protector's numbers, and acted as front-line [[Troop/Legends|troops]] during battle, assaulting the enemy's forward ranks while the Protectors [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacked]] in and struck behind the opponent's lines. The Mandalorian Protectors equipped their Legionnaire droids with ancient [[Mandalorian battle harness/Legends|battle harnesses]], and the droids saw some of the heaviest fighting during the Protectors' campaigns at [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] and [[New Bornalex/Legends|New Bornalex]], only to be nearly [[Mission to Norval II|wiped out]] at [[Norval II]] with the rest of the Protectors. Some survived, however, and during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] reign, [[BL-17|one]] Legionnaire droid was owned by the renowned [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].

==Description==
A [[Class four droid/Legends|class four droid]], the BL-series Battle Legionnaire was designed as a powerful [[Battle droid/Legends|combat droid]]. Each Battle Legionnaire was 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height, and similar in overall physical appearance to the [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series]] of [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]] manufactured by [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]], though the Legionnaire [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was produced by the [[Droid factory|droid foundries]] of [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]].<ref name="NEGTD" />

[[File:BLdroid.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A BL-series Battle Legionnaire droid]]
The Battle Legionnaire droids were primarily adorned with a drab green [[Color/Legends|color]] scheme—matching the color of the [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] common among the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] with which the Legionnaires fought—though portions of their plating were [[blue]], and marked with [[Gold/Legends|gold]]-colored trim. Their two [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] were typically lit with a gold color, as well. BL-series droids had military-grade balance gyros, and their many joints had been calibrated to allow for maximum torque. As a result, Battle Legionnaire droids could lift thousands of [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] above their heads, and run at speeds approaching thirty [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] per [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] for more than a [[Galactic Standard Calendar|day]] without the need for a recharge. The droids were equipped with signal [[Jammer/Legends|jammers]], and carried an internal [[Medpac/Legends|first-aid kit]] within their [[Armor/Legends|armored]] bodies for treating their [[Species/Legends|organic]] comrades.<ref name="NEGTD" />

Battle Legionnaire's were capable of wielding [[Limb/Legends|hand]]-held [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] in combat,<ref name="GoI" /> though they were most often outfitted with [[Death/Legends|deadly]]<ref name="HotM">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref> [[Briletto Company|Briletto]] [[AAP-II blaster box|AAP-11 blaster]] boxes that were strapped to their chests. A number of specialized BL-series droids were armed with ancient [[Mandalorian battle harness/Legends|Mandalorian battle harnesses]], large weapon platforms mounted on twin [[Tank/Legends|tank]] [[Crawler (vehicle)|treads]] and equipped with [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], a heavy [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]], a giant claw arm, and a sonic-pulverizing trip hammer.<ref name="NEGTD" />

==History==
Following the arrival of the former [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC trooper]] known as [[Spar]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]],<ref name="HotM" /> the cultural [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Mandalorian people,<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Spar set out to create an [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Mandalorian Supercommando|Mandalorian supercommandos]]. Drawing from local [[Mandalorian Guard/Legends|police forces]] and former members of the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] splinter group, Spar assembled an army of two-hundred and twelve soldiers known as the [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]]. Spar ascended to the role of [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']],<ref name="HotM" /> the traditional ruler of the Mandalorian clans,<ref name="TMPaC">{{InsiderCite|86|The Mandalorians: People and Culture}}</ref> and aligned his world and people with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in a conflict against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] known as the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="GaW" />

[[File:BattleLegionnaireNR1100-GOI.jpg|thumb|right|225px|A Battle Legionnaire standing beside an [[NR-1100 slicer droid]]]]
Remembering the role the [[Mandallian Giant]]s played in Mandalorian forces during the [[New Sith Wars]], Spar commissioned a [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of droids from their new Separatist allies,<ref name="NEGTD" /> awarded with a run of one-thousand BL-series Battle Legionnaire droids, bolstering the Protectors' meager numbers.<ref name="HotM" /> Designed in part by the Mandalorian Protector [[Fenn Shysa]],<ref name="GaW" /> who was inspired by a run-in he had with the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[C-3PX]],<ref name="NEGTD" /> the BL-series was manufactured in the Separatist droid foundries<ref name="HotM" /> of the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]]-owned company, [[Baktoid Industries/Legends|Baktoid]] Combat Automata. Though the Mandalorians were traditionally ambivalent toward the use of droids,<ref name="NEGTD" /> preferring the benefits of their own hard work,<ref name="ImpCom501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref> the Battle Legionnaire droids were afforded a level of respect similar to the [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] used by the ancient [[Mandalorian Crusaders]]. During the Mandalorian Protectors' engagements, the Battle Legionnaires saw some of the heaviest fighting;<ref name="NEGTD" /> Spar would often use the BL-series droids as heavy front-line units, attacking the enemies forward ranks while the Protectors themselves would [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] in behind their opponents' defenses and strike with the element of surprise.<ref name="GaW" /> Several Battle Legionnaire droids were also equipped with Mandalorian battle harnesses, using the deadly weapons platforms to deal out more devastating damage.<ref name="NEGTD" /> Because of their effectiveness, the Mandalorian Protectors and their Battle Legionnaire droids became highly sought-after Separatist units, with a reputation for taking down a target in a day that the Confederacy's [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|droid army]] couldn't in a [[Standard month/Legends|month]].<ref name="GaW" />

The BL-series droids took part in the Mandalorian Protectors' [[Battle of Kamino (Separatist–Mandalorian invasion)|strike]] against the planet [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]<ref name="NEGTD" /> and the Republic [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]] facilities located there, as part of a series of blitzkrieg assaults in retaliation for the death of a prominent Confederacy leader.<ref name="HotM" /> Prior to the [[Battle of New Bornalex|assault]] on [[New Bornalex/Legends|New Bornalex]], the Protectors toasted their Battle Legionnaire droids,<ref name="NEGTD" /> and though the battle was a victory for the Protectors,<ref name="HotM" /> their army of BL-series droids suffered significant casualties.<ref name="NEGTD" /> On [[Norval II]], during an [[Mission to Norval II|attempt]] to capture the Republic [[Senator/Legends|senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], the Mandalorian Protectors were led into a trap and ambushed by the Republic's [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]].<ref name="HotM" /> The vast majority of the Mandalorian Protectors were killed in the ensuing conflict, and all but a few Battle Legionnaire droids shared their fate.<ref name="NEGTD" />

One of the few Legionnaire droids to survive, [[BL-17]], was acquired by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], and was put to work as the<ref name="NEGTD" /> culturally estranged Mandalorian's<ref name="Bloodlines">''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]''</ref> aide during the early days of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] reign.<ref name="NEGTD" /> However, BL-17 came to an end on the planet [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]]<ref name="ARttF">{{DroidsCite|A Race to the Finish}}</ref> in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],<ref name="Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO">{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}}</ref> following a skirmish with the droids [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[Proto One]].<ref name="ARttF" /> In the years after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], rumors floated about of a belligerent [[8t88/Legends|88-series droid]] that possessed the head from a BL-series droid.<ref name="Katarn'sTale">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale4|series=Kyle Katarn's Tale|text=Mission 4}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The BL-series Battle Legionnaire droid was first introduced in the [[Star Wars Insider]] article, [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']], written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and published in the magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 80|eightieth issue]] in [[March]] of [[2005]]. The article [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] the appearance of the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars Droids'']] character BL-17 as a member of the Battle Legionnaire line, and detailed the history of the droids, including their connection to the Mandalorians. The Battle Legionnaire droids received further mentions in [[Daniel Wallace]]'s ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' and the ''[[Galaxy at War]]'' sourcebook, in addition to ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
